title: Functional case for good price
text: It works fine for what it is--a case, and for under $5 I completely recommend it. It is not a hard shell but it does have some rigidity on parts of the case where the raised design is. Storage for 3 cards (I have doubled slots to accommodate a total of 6) and a stylus. Slight extra room around 3DS but no real wobbling upon shaking.

title: Audio dropouts on PS4 and XBOX
text: The sound is very good in game, however I get frequent audio dropouts in the game and chat sound on 2 different sets of GSP 300's. I tried them on 2 different XBox X's and PS4 Pros. One set has slightly more dropouts than the other so I will definitely return that one and maybe both. I tried other headphones which did not produce this issue. Material is nice and they are comfortable but it is hard to recommend them as I do not know if these came from a bad batch or it is a frequent problem.

title: Super comfortable and good sound
text: Using this as a wired headset on XBOX and PS4. I was hesitant to try them a many reviewers said the sennheiser game one was super comfortable and sounded great and that the Cloud Alpha was uncomfortable when worn for a while due to their ears hitting the drivers. I found these to be way more comfortable than the Game One's as the memory foam is extremely soft and they feel great even after a few hours. The Game Ones were less comfortable at first but they are OK after a while but I did not find their ear cushions nearly as good a the Cloud Alpha's. The Game Ones sound was slightly clearer with slightly cleaner mids but they were not worth the extra money for gaming. I got the Cloud Alpha for 79.99 and the Game One for 133.00. Of course, sound is a very personal thing but these were the best choice for me.

title: AMAZING
text: Amazing. Love the poster and controller skin I received with it. Five stars. Excellent gameplay and close to real-life graphics. 2K does it again.

title: Excellent! Works great, looks good, fair price!
text: Came across this product just scrolling through the Video Game category, and it was a great purchase. It seems fairly durable, looks good, and seems to charge my two controllers correctly. This helps keeps my room clean and looking organized, as the controllers are put back on the stand when I'm done playing PS4. I usually forget or am too lazy to charge my controllers, but this makes it so easy to do and I no longer use my actual phone charger to charge my controllers. The fit on the controller is great, as the controller just slides right into its spot in order to charge. I really recommend this product for any PS4 players. It's a very fair price, and a great value. I'll give it 5 stars.

title: Pretty good game
text: Good game for about $20. Keeps me busy, haven't dove into it too much but it's nothing spectacular but also nothing bad. I enjoy it- it's a time killer and includes some thrills throughout the game. I'll give it 4/5 stars.

title: Cool Retro
text: A lot of cool Retro Games into a Cool Little Package. Screen is nice, and the sound is really loud. I keep it on my desk, so USB Power is good enough. You could plug it into a power bank if you’re on the go. If you want to connect it to your tv or plug it into the wall, you’ll have to buy those separate. If you would rather have a pad controller you can buy one of those separate too. Getting 40 games is a pretty good deal for $50.

title: Loose
text: Great build quality, and long cord. Only bad thing is that the control stick is really loose.

title: Nice keyboard
text: I was worried after hearing that the Firmware might brick it. I made sure I had the latest iCUE installed. When I did the firmware it didn't do anything after install, so I reset my PC, and tried again. Second time it told me to plug in my keyboard, so I had to unplug, and plugged it back in. I tried the firmware again, and it worked no problems. My Motherboard is an ASUS Maximus X Code. The keyboard feels good. I like the textured keys, and I also like all the extra keys that come with it. I like the wrist rest it's better than the plastic one on my old Logitech G910. The lighting is really bright. I also had no problems getting into the Bios when my PC Starts. I only marked it down because I had to resort to YouTube to figure how to customize the lighting, and I figure out the Macros on my own. I'm happy with my purchase.

title: Awesome!
text: The G903 was a really good mouse, but I like that the G502 was smaller. I can now reach the top side button easier unlike the G903. In Destiny 2 I have one lower set for melee, and the top for Finisher. I didn’t need the weights, because it felt good without them. Great battery life, but you don’t need to even worry about that stuff if you have the Powerplay Pad. Only problem I had was getting the Poweplay Pad to work wirelessly with the G502. I just reinstalled GHUB, and removed my old G903, and I was good to go. Very happy with the G502.

title: Great controller at a great price
text: Great controller at a great price. Very solidly built and very comfortable in the hand. Materials are cheaper than OEM, but it's way cheaper so what do you expect? Buttons work well, but the D-pad is a bit squishy. Triggers actually have shorter throws than the OEM so I think this is great for fps games. Overall very worth the money and I would highly recommend.

title: Great cheap chat headset (Since the XBOX Ones don't seem ...
text: Great cheap chat headset (Since the XBOX Ones don't seem to come with them anymore). This should have been included in the box from Microsoft. Comfortable and functions very well. Sound isn't mind blowing, it's a one ear chat headset what do you expect? Perfect for what it is supposed to be and does the job it needs to do. Would recommend.

title: Great quality!
text: Great build quality and plenty of features. Wonderful remote.

title: Awesome game!

title: Generally love it, could be better though.
text: In general I love this controller, however it could be better. My biggest two complaints are the z button and the joystick. It is very hard to get used to z being in the r2/l2 position. I think keeping a behind the controller z button would have been best. Also the joystick is waaaaay too sensitive. This should definitely be dialed back several ticks. However, overall, I do like the product.

title: Wanted the more expensive Razer Controller, but this one works perfectly...
text: I wanted to get the more expensive Razer controller that lights up, but because that was out of my proce range, I had to go with this one. So far, after having this for 3 months, I've used it a lot and it is fun. I really like it. I use it on PC, and I know with FPS games, Keyboard and Mouse is better, But I've always liked controller better. I like how the buttons feel, and i like the little customization that it offers. Good job Razer!

title: When the cable stops working well, you can just ...
text: When the cable stops working well, you can just buy another cable. Thats the beat part about this compared to the other models of this headset.

title: The Zombies mode on this game is the best Zombies I have ever played
text: I have played Zombies at my friends house and this is the only reason I wanted this game. The Zombies mode on this game is the best Zombies I have ever played.

title: Hurts a little, but overall beautiful
text: If you're a razer stan like me, then you'll love this. I'm going for a Quartz/Mercury combo gaming setup so this fits perfectly into it. My only problem with this is that I usually have one side behind my ears and it isn't the most comfortable. If it was wireless, I wouldn't care as much. The Sony WH1000xm3/4 are better for sitting one side behind your ears but besides that, I love it. Great price. Not too expensive and not too cheap.

title: Wireless? Yes sir/ma'am
text: Like any razer keyboard, it's got RGB lighting. Amazing for gaming. Has a dongle to connect to the computer wirelessly and can connect via Bluetooth. The bluetooth doesn't last as long and needs to be charged, which luckily, it can be used when connected via wire. It uses USB C for charging, which in my opinion, makes it a more advanced keyboard.

title: Smaller than a regular keyboard
text: I wanted a keyboard for keycaps. My old keyboard was a razer too, but it was mechamenbrane. Couldn't do keycaps. If this was a full sized keyboard, it would get a full 5 stars, but I knew what I was buying when I bought it. I bought it just because it was cheap and for the keys.

title: System versus the Games
text: Biggest problems is that higher textured games have more glitches. My system is way more capable than these games offer.  I have Intel I7,  16gb GDDR,  256gb SSD,  1tb 7200RPM sata,  NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060. 1800FSB .  Games like Arizona Sunshine, horrible for glitching during game play.  Jungle Dino never could get it to even load from main menu using the Oculus touch controllers.  So there si still a lot of work to be done with these units or games.  I wanted skyrim, no customer support on how to load this pc game and do the settings. None!  I thought it was going to be plug and play, but I find myself constantly having to tweak settings per game.  I had to get refunds on a few titles, just couldn't get them to work smoothly,  I run two sensors, one behind and one front, gives a great 180 view. No issues.  I think its a over all nice unit, still some bugs to work out. As time goes maybe they can resolve these glitches.
